```
tests/test_kernelmanager.py::TestKernelManager::test_signal_kernel_subprocesses
```
A recent example CI run is https://github.com/ipython/ipykernel/actions/runs/10416809602/job/28849785992.

The failure occurs in
https://github.com/jupyter/jupyter_client/blob/8ca76ea1e67c9732a67cab812f8129b68671b9cd/tests/signalkernel.py#L50-L55
because we can no longer catch a `KeyboardInterrupt` interrupt in this way as anyio's support for this requires it to be implemented in a specific way as documented at https://anyio.readthedocs.io/en/stable/signals.html#handling-keyboardinterrupt-and-systemexit to support all the various async backends.

The problem occurs in test code only, so this PR changes that test code so for ipykernel >= 7 it uses the recommended anyio solution and otherwise falls back to the current implementation. This passes locally for me on both Linux and macOS. There is no explicit testing of jupyter_client CI against ipykernel main branch, so to test this we either just merge it and rerun ipykernel's downstream tests, or we have to add an extra temporary CI to use ipykernel main branch.

There may well be other failures in CI once this passes as it currently stops on this failure. I see different subsequent failures on Linux and macOS so we will have to see what happens in {"action":"opened","number":14044,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/pulls/14044","id":2040405705,"node_id":"PR_kwDOADqB3M55nh7J","html_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14044","diff_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14044.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14044.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14044","number":14044,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"WebGL rendering of Ngon glyph","user":{"login":"ianthomas23","id":580326,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU4MDMyNg==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/580326?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23","html_url":"https://github.com/ianthomas23","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This implements WebGL rendering of the new Ngon glyph.

Here is the new visual test image, you can tell by the hatching pattern that WebGL is used on the right:

![Glyph_models__should_support_Ngon](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9e9b7067-24a1-4251-b8f1-d073495fa93a)

In terms of implementation I have moved most of the existing `CircleGL` into its new base class `RadialGL` which is also the base class for the new `NgonGL` class. For the GLSL shaders I have reused the existing marker shaders adding a new marker type `"ngon"`. This allows us to reuse the hatching, line joins, etc. The only thing not supported is line dashes, which is not supported for any of the markers.

For the new visual test I've had to add a new `Figure.ngon` function to `glyph_api.ts`. I haven't done this before so I've copied, pasted and modified the `circle` code for this. Someone with more experience of this should probably check that I have done this correctly.

- [x] issues: fixes #14035
- [x] tests added / passed

rendering of Ngon glyph","user":{"login":"ianthomas23","id":580326,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU4MDMyNg==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/580326?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23","html_url":"https://github.com/ianthomas23","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This implements WebGL rendering of the new Ngon glyph.

Here is the new visual test image, you can tell by the hatching pattern that WebGL is used on the right:

![Glyph_models__should_support_Ngon](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9e9b7067-24a1-4251-b8f1-d073495fa93a)

In terms of implementation I have moved most of the existing `CircleGL` into its new base class `RadialGL` which is also the base class for the new `NgonGL` class. For the GLSL shaders I have reused the existing marker shaders adding a new marker type `"ngon"`. This allows us to reuse the hatching, line joins, etc. The only thing not supported is line dashes, which is not supported for any of the markers.

For the new visual test I've had to add a new `Figure.ngon` function to `glyph_api.ts`. I haven't done this before so I've copied, pasted and modified the `circle` code for this. Someone with more experience of this should probably check that I have done this correctly.

- [x] issues: fixes #14035
- [x] tests added / passed

{"action":"closed","number":14044,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/pulls/14044","id":2040405705,"node_id":"PR_kwDOADqB3M55nh7J","html_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14044","diff_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14044.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/pull/14044.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/bokeh/bokeh/issues/14044","number":14044,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"WebGL rendering of Ngon glyph","user":{"login":"ianthomas23","id":580326,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU4MDMyNg==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/580326?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23","html_url":"https://github.com/ianthomas23","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This implements WebGL rendering of the new Ngon glyph.

Here is the new visual test image, you can tell by the hatching pattern that WebGL is used on the right:

![Glyph_models__should_support_Ngon](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9e9b7067-24a1-4251-b8f1-d073495fa93a)

In terms of implementation I have moved most of the existing `CircleGL` into its new base class `RadialGL` which is also the base class for the new `NgonGL` class. For the GLSL shaders I have reused the existing marker shaders adding a new marker type `"ngon"`. This allows us to reuse the hatching, line joins, etc. The only thing not supported is line dashes, which is not supported for any of the markers.

For the new visual test I've had to add a new `Figure.ngon` function to `glyph_api.ts`. I haven't done this before so I've copied, pasted and modified the `circle` code for this. Someone with more experience of this should probably check that I have done this correctly.

- [x] issues: fixes #14035
- [x] tests added / passed

#1124 which is that the use of `%matplotlib osx` crashes `ipykernel` and downstream libraries whereas `ipython` is fine.

The fix is to specify the `argtypes` of each `objc_msgSend` call just before the call. This is consistent with how `ipython` successfully handles it. I have tested this manually using `spyder` and `jupyter console`, `qtconsole`, `lab` and `nbclassic`: using `ipykernel 6.29.4` (the latest release) they all crash, using this branch they all work as expected.

This PR is opened against the `6.x` branch rather than `main` as issue #1235 means it is not possible to test any `matplotlib` GUI loops on `main`. It would be good to get this into a release (6.29.5?) as soon as possible and not have to wait for #1235 and the other AnyIO fallout issues to be fixed beforehand. This PR will therefore need to be forward ported to `main`.

There is no explicit test for this as you need to actually render a macos window to trigger the failure, and this is not an easy thing to do on headless CI. Essentially this functionality sits in the grey area between `matplotlib` and `ipython/ipykernel/jupyter` rather than in any individual project.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipykernel/issues/1237/reactions","total_count":3,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipykernel/issues/1237/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipykernel/issues/comments/2200256206","html_url":"https://github.com/ipython/ipykernel/pull/1237#issuecomment-2200256206","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/ipython/ipykernel/issues/1237","id":2200256206,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAgGK786DJT7O","user":{"login":"ianthomas23","id":580326,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU4MDMyNg==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/580326?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23","html_url":"https://github.com/ianthomas23","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ianthomas23/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-07-01T14:09:14Z","updated_at":"2024-07-01T14:09:14Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"@blink1073 Done!
